high.observable_symptom: Creating a SASI index with an illegal argument combination writes the index to the schema tables before instantiating the tokenizer; the resulting RuntimeException then recurs on boot so the node fails to start when loading the schema.

Symptom is a failed startup, not a mere error-type swap. Reproducer is derivable (CREATE SASI index with bad args, then restart); a PoC commit/unit test is linked.

high.observable_symptom: Pending ranges are not recalculated when a keyspace is created during bootstrap, so writes for the new keyspace never reach the joining node; after bootstrap finishes the node has no data for that keyspace (silent data loss / quorum can miss the mutation).

Multi-node bootstrap data-loss defect.

high.observable_symptom: Short read protection miscomputes limits for GROUP BY queries (uses row count instead of group count), returning a stale/deleted row: 'Expect no live data, but got [0, 0]' — a wrong query result.

medium.observable_symptom: clients refuse to connect to a restarted node: native protocol capped to V3 so the driver reports 'does not support the version V4 of the native protocol which is currently in use'

medium.reason: Real client-visible symptom on a single version: 'Detected peers which do not fully support protocol V4. Capping max negotiable version to V3' though 'ALL the nodes ... were apache-cassandra-3.11.6'; caused by a joining node's NULL peers-version failing the check. Single-version, multi-node (joining + restart); mechanism derivable.

medium.observable_symptom: spurious disk failure policy invocation when a hint file has a run of trailing zeros (e.g. after a hard reboot), instead of safely ignoring the trailing zeros
medium.one_line_trigger: hint file with trailing zeros (unflushed/hard-reboot) replayed -> disk failure policy triggered spuriously
medium.reason: Operator-visible fault handling: trailing zeros in hint files 'can be safely ignored and we should avoid invoking the disk failure policy in these cases'. Can be reproduced on one node by fabricating a hint file with trailing zeros and triggering replay rather than needing an actual crash, so single-node/derivable.

medium.observable_symptom: nodetool garbagecollect on an LCS table rewrites all output to L0, causing extreme write amplification, large temporary disk-space spikes, and broken LCS level/timestamp structure.
medium.one_line_trigger: nodetool garbagecollect on a LeveledCompactionStrategy table -> all data dumped to L0 + write amplification
medium.reason: Body: 'nodetool garbagecollect always outputs to L0 with LeveledCompactionStrategy ... all data is in L0 ... awful sequence of useless temporary space usage and write amplification'. Task says nodetool that triggers real DB behavior (compaction/garbagecollect) IS db_behavior; reproducer is derivable (LCS table + garbagecollect) though no verbatim script.

medium.observable_symptom: Wrong/surprising query results: after DROP COMPACT STORAGE, cached prepared statements are not invalidated, so a prepared query keeps returning the old column set until the statement is re-prepared.
medium.one_line_trigger: prepare a statement, ALTER ... DROP COMPACT STORAGE, re-execute prepared statement -> stale column set returned
medium.reason: Body: DROP COMPACT STORAGE 'might change the number of columns ... the query will not return the correct set of columns until the statement has been invalidate ... the resultset change at unexpected point in time'. Single-node, reproducible via prepare + DROP COMPACT STORAGE + re-execute (derivable, no verbatim script).

medium.observable_symptom: Node fails to join the cluster with 'Unable to gossip with any seeds' when listen_address is changed while prefer_local is enabled, because peers keep contacting the old local address during the shadow round.
medium.one_line_trigger: enable prefer_local + change a node's listen_address + restart -> node fails to join with 'Unable to gossip with any seeds'
medium.reason: Body: with prefer_local enabled 'nodes were unable to join the cluster and fail with Unable to gossip with any seeds'. Mechanism (existing node talks to stale local address during shadow round) is derivable on a multi-node cluster but no verbatim script is given.

medium.observable_symptom: CorruptSSTableExceptions caused by ALTER TABLE-introduced column placeholders being read-repaired into the memtable, flushed, and conflicting with the actual column (e.g. a collection)
medium.one_line_trigger: ALTER TABLE ADD column (collection) + read-repair of column placeholder into memtable + flush -> CorruptSSTableException
medium.reason: Body states the placeholders 'can get read-repaired in to the memtable and later flushed to disk and in some cases this can conflict with the actual column (if the actual column is a collection for example) and cause CorruptSSTableExceptions.' No verbatim script but schema+operation+condition are described; read-repair divergence is stageable via gossip isolation on a ring.

medium.observable_symptom: Bogus unavailable responses to clients because peers retain pending ranges for a gracefully-shutdown (SHUTDOWN) node that was involved in a MOVE and has left the ring
medium.one_line_trigger: MOVE operation + graceful shutdown of moving node -> stale pending ranges -> bogus unavailable responses to clients
medium.reason: Body: 'This in turn can lead to bogus unavailable responses to clients if a replica for the any of the pending ranges should go down'; mechanism (MOVE + graceful SHUTDOWN leaving pending ranges) is described and stageable on a ring with nodetool move + shutdown, though no verbatim script is given.

medium.observable_symptom: Unnecessary truncation snapshots are created for system.table_estimates and system.size_estimates on every node startup, leaking snapshot disk usage
medium.one_line_trigger: restart node + nodetool listsnapshots -> spurious truncated-* snapshots of size_estimates/table_estimates each restart
medium.reason: In-body repro (stop/start node, then 'nodetool listsnapshots') shows 'truncation snapshots are created for the tables system.table_estimates and system.size_estimates' on each restart, an operator-visible snapshot/disk leak reproducible on one node.

medium.observable_symptom: An empty TOC.txt file (from an unclean shutdown) causes an exception on startup when taking a snapshot, because it tries to acquire 0 permits from the rate limiter
medium.one_line_trigger: empty (0-byte) TOC.txt for an sstable + take snapshot -> exception acquiring 0 permits from rate limiter
medium.reason: Body: 'we can end up with empty TOC files during unclean shutdown, then, on startup we get an exception when trying to acquire 0 permits from the rate limiter.' The precondition is a file state (empty TOC.txt), not a timing race, so it is deterministically stageable on one node (truncate TOC.txt, then snapshot).

medium.observable_symptom: Bootstrap failure: with strict consistency and RF > endpoint count, RangeStreamer selects all endpoints as sources so 'the bootstrapping node is almost guaranteed to fail in the validation later.'
medium.one_line_trigger: Bootstrap a new node with strict consistency and RF > number of endpoints -> RangeStreamer picks multiple full sources -> bootstrap fails validation
medium.reason: db_behavior + observable: 'the bootstrapping node is almost guaranteed to fail in the validation later' when RF > endpoints count under strict consistency. Reproducible on a small ring by setting RF above the node count and bootstrapping a node; derivable from the described schema/condition, no verbatim script.

medium.observable_symptom: Data corruption: the standalone scrubber never validates the partition key, so the bad key 'will propagate to the new sstable so it will be corrupted when read later.'
medium.one_line_trigger: Run standalone scrub on an sstable with an invalid partition key -> bad key copied through -> resulting sstable corrupt and fails when read
medium.reason: db_behavior + observable: scrub 'gets the key from the file but never validates it, and this will propagate to the new sstable so it will be corrupted when read later.' Single-node via sstablescrub, but lower confidence because constructing an sstable with an invalid partition key is the hard part; mechanism is derivable from the body.

medium.observable_symptom: Server exception returned to client: 'ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: 1 at FunctionResource.fromName' logged as 'Unexpected exception during request' when handling a function with 0 args.
medium.one_line_trigger: Reference/grant permission on a user function with zero arguments -> FunctionResource.fromName throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
medium.reason: db_behavior + observable: 'FunctionResource can't handle functions with 0 args where it throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: 1' (a stack trace is in the body). Triggered by a VALID zero-arg function through the auth path; reproducible single-node by creating a 0-arg UDF and exercising FunctionResource (e.g. via GRANT/permissions). Mechanism derivable, no verbatim script.

medium.observable_symptom: a non-qualified prepared statement gets executed against the wrong keyspace (wrong-keyspace query results)
medium.one_line_trigger: prepare a non-qualified statement, then execute on a different keyspace -> statement runs against the wrong keyspace
medium.reason: Body: the CASSANDRA-15252 fix 'introduced a regression: non-qualified statements can get prepared against one keyspace but then executed on another under some circumstances'. That is a real CQL-semantics correctness defect reproducible on one node via the driver/cqlsh by preparing then switching keyspaces; no verbatim script, so derivable / medium.

medium.observable_symptom: 'Unexpected disk state: failed to read transaction log' error from divergent transaction-log replicas after anticompaction
medium.one_line_trigger: anticompaction-after-repair with 2 data dirs where one new sstable writer is empty/aborted -> lazily-created log replica missing an ADD -> log replica mismatch error
medium.reason: Body shows the 'Unexpected disk state: failed to read transaction log' error with one replica 'missing an ADD entry' and gives a detailed 6-step mechanism (trackNew in /tmp/d1, aborted empty writer, lazy log creation in /tmp/d0). Reproducible on one node with 2 data directories during anticompaction; precise key-distribution condition makes it derivable / medium.

medium.observable_symptom: node takes over 30 minutes to process system.repairs before it can restart (exponential slowdown)
medium.one_line_trigger: many vnodes + range-split incremental repairs populate system.repairs -> node restart processing takes 30+ minutes
medium.reason: Operator-visible startup regression: 'causes a node to take over 30 minutes to process the data stored in the system.repairs table before the node can restart' due to RepairState.add re-sorting the whole list each insert ('exponential growth in processing time'); mechanism (vnodes + range splits + incremental repair) is described plus an attached unit test, but reproduction needs a populated repairs table.

medium.observable_symptom: sstableloader sends plaintext on the internode port and streaming fails with 'connection closed' against an SSL-only node
medium.one_line_trigger: sstableloader with server_encryption_options configured -> ignores TLS, sends plaintext, connection closed by SSL-only server
medium.reason: Operator-visible: 'sstableloader should perform an SSL handshake ... But this does not happen ... sstableloader sends plaintext traffic', and 'Streaming phase of the migration failed with a connection closed error'; the yaml encryption config and tcpdump bytes are in the body, though reproduction needs a TLS/keystore setup and a target node.

medium.observable_symptom: CL=ONE counter writes keep timing out because clients keep routing to a network-partitioned node (until partition removed)
medium.one_line_trigger: CL=ONE writes to a counter table + one node partitioned via iptables -> partitioned node keeps receiving traffic and timing out (vs C3 which stops)
medium.reason: Operator/client-visible steady state: 'the partitioned node keeps receiving traffic until the iptables rules are removed' with sustained 50-70% timeouts, vs Cassandra 3 where 'the partitioned node stops receiving traffic'; full ccm+iptables+app.py repro is in the body and the failure is a held-partition steady state (not a precise timing window), so it is stageable at kubectl level.

medium.observable_symptom: snapshot created when dropping a materialized view lacks the 'dropped-' name prefix
medium.one_line_trigger: auto_snapshot:true + DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW -> snapshot directory name missing 'dropped-' prefix
medium.reason: Operator-visible naming defect: 'When auto_snapshot: true and MV is dropped, the name of the snapshot does not start with "dropped-" prefix as a normal table would'; 3.11.x only, and the trigger (enable auto_snapshot, drop an MV, inspect snapshot name) is derivable and single-node.

medium.observable_symptom: IllegalArgumentException 'newLimit > capacity' returned to client on paged SELECT during a mixed-version rolling upgrade
medium.one_line_trigger: paged SELECT during 3.11.12->4.0.3 rolling upgrade -> PagingState clustering decode IllegalArgumentException 'newLimit > capacity'
medium.reason: Client-visible error during upgrade: 'IllegalArgumentException: newLimit > capacity: (290 > 15)' in PagingState.decodeClustering on a paged SELECT, seen 'On upgraded 4.0.3 nodes' and 'disappeared once all 6 nodes have been on 4.0.3'; mechanism (mixed-version cluster + paged query) is described but needs a specific mixed-version ring to reproduce.

medium.observable_symptom: writetime()/ttl() on a single-cell frozen collection is wrongly rejected with an error
medium.one_line_trigger: SELECT writetime(frozen_set_column) -> wrongly fails
medium.reason: Per the in-body example, 'SELECT writetime(st)' on a frozen<set<int>> column goes from '-- fails' to '-- allowed' after the fix: a valid query on a single-cell frozen collection is wrongly rejected. The coexisting hardening (unfrozen UDT now rejected) does not make this 'none' because legitimate frozen-collection writetime is a client-visible behavior change. In-body CQL schema + selects, single-node.

medium.observable_symptom: nodetool enablebinary fails on a join_ring=false gossiping-only member, so the native transport cannot be re-enabled after disablebinary
medium.one_line_trigger: start with -Dcassandra.join_ring=false, nodetool disablebinary, then enablebinary -> throws (state STARTING, not NORMAL)
medium.reason: Operator-visible defect with a clean single-node reproducer: 'If we disable it via "nodetool disablebinary", it is not possible to enable it back with "nodetool enablebinary"' because checkServiceAllowedToStart throws since the gossiping-only member is 'STARTING for ever'. enablebinary triggers real native-transport (re)start behavior; steps fully in-body.

medium.observable_symptom: IndexOutOfBoundsException returned when a V3 paging state is deserialized on a V4 connection (integer overflow of the index field)
medium.one_line_trigger: resume paging with a V3 PagingState over a V4 native-protocol connection -> integer overflow -> IndexOutOfBoundsException
medium.reason: Client-visible defect on legitimate protocol-version skew (title: 'V3 State Received on V4 Connection'): in PagingState.java 'we add long values to it without a Math.toIntExact check' so a large vint 'would cause integer overflow', surfacing an IndexOutOfBoundsException to the client. Reproducible on one node by feeding a V3-format paging state over a V4 connection; mechanism (schema-free, protocol+operation+condition) is derivable.

medium.observable_symptom: all triggers fail to load (trigger mechanism broken in-server)
medium.one_line_trigger: load a trigger from a JAR on 4.1.0 -> CustomClassLoader fails to copy JAR (Files.copy won't overwrite) -> trigger does not load
medium.reason: Body states 'the trigger mechanism does not work, not their trigger, but the possibility of loading any trigger', because CustomClassLoader switched to java.nio Files.copy which won't overwrite the pre-created temp file. Triggers run in-server, so db_behavior; repro derivable but needs building a trigger JAR.

medium.observable_symptom: with disk_failure_policy=die the JVM is not killed (gossip/transport stay up) on FS error
medium.one_line_trigger: disk_failure_policy=die + inject filesystem error -> Cassandra only throws instead of shutting down gossip/transport and killing the JVM
medium.reason: Body: 'when encounter a file system error, Cassandra server will only throw exception instead of shut down gossip and client transport and kill JVM' because the default FS error handler mishandles policy 'die'. Operator-visible (node fails to die as documented); single-node but needs FS-error injection.

medium.observable_symptom: hints are stored even though hinted_handoff_enabled=false
medium.one_line_trigger: set hinted_handoff_enabled=false + write with a replica down/timing out -> hints still submitted (config not honored)
medium.reason: Body: CASSANDRA-17164 moved hint submission to AbstractWriteResponseHandler.onFailure which 'no longer checks if CallbackInfo.shouldHint ... which ultimately checks if hinted_handoff_enabled=true', so hints are stored when disabled. Needs a replica down so the write-failure path fires; check hints are created.

medium.observable_symptom: SSTables deleted (data loss) when a compaction FSReadError is actually an OutOfMemoryError under best_effort policy
medium.one_line_trigger: best_effort disk_failure_policy + mmap 'Map failed' OOM during compaction + restart -> unfinished-txn SSTables deleted (data loss)
medium.reason: Body shows FSReadError caused by 'java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Map failed' during compaction, then 'Restart the node, Verifying logfile transaction, All sstables are deleted' via LogTransaction 'Unfinished transaction log, deleting' — i.e. data loss. Severe and server-side, but repro needs forcing mmap exhaustion (e.g. max_map_count) plus restart.

medium.observable_symptom: nodetool repair --paxos-only runs anti-compactions and hangs (treated as incremental repair)
medium.one_line_trigger: nodetool repair --paxos-only -> triggers anti-compactions / stalls instead of finishing quickly (works only with --full --paxos-only)
medium.reason: Body: 'nodetool repair --paxos-only ... triggered multiple anti-compactions on the nodes' and does not complete quickly, whereas '--full --paxos-only ... Completes within a few seconds'. Repair/anti-compaction is real server behavior (rubric NOTE on nodetool triggering DB behavior); the two nodetool commands are in-body, needs a ring.

medium.observable_symptom: Deleted data is resurrected (tombstones incorrectly pruned during partial compaction)
medium.one_line_trigger: disk-full partial compaction drops largest sstable while tombstone overlap is stale -> deleted data resurrected
medium.reason: Real data-correctness defect: 'This leads to the faulty conclusion that the tombstones can be pruned during the compaction, causing the data to be resurrected.' Mechanism (schema + tombstones in newer sstable + disk-full forcing partial compaction) is derivable, though the disk-full condition is the staging challenge.

medium.observable_symptom: Native transport accepts queries before peers marked live, causing LOCAL_QUORUM consistency-level failures after a node restart
medium.one_line_trigger: restart node in multi-node cluster -> native transport starts before peers seen live -> LOCAL_QUORUM query errors
medium.reason: Operator-visible defect: 'native transport starts it will receive queries and fail consistency levels such as LOCAL_QUORUM since it still thinks nodes are down ... a node restart is causing a bunch of query errors.' Needs a ring and a restart; mechanism is derivable but the timing of the settle window adds uncertainty.

medium.observable_symptom: NPE returned to client when invoking a UDF after a node restart (keyspace metadata loaded as null during schema load)
medium.one_line_trigger: restart node with UDF referencing a UDT -> getKeyspaceMetadata returns null during schema load -> NPE on UDF call
medium.reason: Client-visible failure: 'NPE thrown when used after restart' because 'the keyspace for the UDF name is being constructed and is not yet in the instance so the method returns null for the KeyspaceMetadata.' This is a single-node startup schema-load-order bug (rolling restart is just how it was hit); a minimal UDT+UDF+restart repro is constructible from the described mechanism (cql files referenced).

medium.observable_symptom: memory leak (completed incremental-repair coordinator sessions are never freed) leading to heap growth / OOM on long-lived nodes
medium.one_line_trigger: Run many incremental repairs over long uptime -> CoordinatorSessions.sessions never cleaned -> memory leak
medium.reason: Body: 'nothing cleaning up repaired sessions from ...CoordinatorSessions#sessions. This causes memory to leak for cluster members with long uptimes and lots of incremental repair' (Consistency/Repair). Operator-visible leak derivable from repeated incremental repair on a ring, though it only manifests at scale/over time.

medium.observable_symptom: spurious startup WARN 'Timed out after 10002 milliseconds, was waiting for remaining peers to connect' and ~10s startup delay when a 4.x node joins a 3.x cluster
medium.one_line_trigger: Start a 4.x node against a 3.x cluster -> StartupClusterConnectivityChecker times out waiting on PING (not in 3.x) -> WARN + startup delay
medium.reason: Body shows the operator-visible WARN 'Timed out after 10002 milliseconds, was waiting for remaining peers to connect' on 'Starting up a new 4.X node on a 3.x cluster' because 'the PING messages used by the startup check are not available on 3.X' (Local/Startup and Shutdown). Reproduction needs a mixed 3.x/4.x ring (more than a single-image swap), so multi-node-stageable with that caveat.

medium.observable_symptom: read requests time out (100% timeout) at LOCAL_QUORUM during node addition when blocking read repair sends a mutation to a remote-DC node
medium.one_line_trigger: 2-DC cluster + add node + LOCAL_QUORUM read-after-write load -> blocking read repair picks remote-DC node -> latch never counts down -> read timeout
medium.reason: Operator-visible defect: 'this will cause 100% timeout' / 'You will see read timeout issue from time to time'; reproducer is derivable ('reproduced if you have a constant load on a 3 + 3 cluster when adding a node ... use local_quorum consistency with a constant read after write load'). Needs a multi-DC ring + bootstrap, stageable.

medium.observable_symptom: all native-transport-request threads become serialized (latency/throughput collapse) on a node restart after a corrupted gossip entry was created by assassinate
medium.one_line_trigger: nodetool assassinate <ip not in gossip> -> corrupted endpointStateMap entry -> restart -> native-transport threads serialize on ExpiringMemoizingSupplier lock
medium.reason: Operator-visible perf collapse triggered by a real nodetool command on real gossip state: 'all the native-transport-requests thread is serialized ... if a node in a cluster with the corrupted gossip map is restart, the node will run into this problem'; derivable from steps ('nodetool assassinate 10.1.1.1' then observe via 'nodetool gossipinfo' and restart). Needs a ring.

medium.observable_symptom: repair coordinator runs out of memory (OOM kills the node) during a full (non -pr) repair
medium.one_line_trigger: full repair (no -pr) on RF=3 + vnodes cluster -> RF*RF simultaneous validation requests accumulate Merkle trees > repair_session_space -> OOM
medium.reason: Operator-visible crash: 'the repair coordinator can send more than RF or RF*RF simultaneous validation requests ... greatly exceeding repair_session_space and OOMing the node'; derivable from a concrete config ('11-node cluster with RF=3 and 256 tokens ... 44*3=132 validation requests'). Needs a multi-node ring + repair, stageable.

medium.observable_symptom: SAI queries fail (do not return wrong results, but fail) when a coordinator treats an UNKNOWN-status replica index as queryable
medium.one_line_trigger: SAI query routed to replica with UNKNOWN index status (not yet propagated) instead of BUILD_SUCCEEDED replicas -> query fails
medium.reason: Operator/client-visible availability defect: an UNKNOWN replica 'will not return incorrect results, but it will fail' and the fix is to 'ignore UNKNOWN replicas and maximize availability'; derivable from mechanism (SAI index status not yet propagated to coordinator). Needs a ring with SAI status gossip, stageable.

medium.observable_symptom: silent data loss on point-in-time restore: sub-second part of restore_point_in_time is truncated, so updates between the second boundary and the requested microsecond are missing
medium.one_line_trigger: set restore_point_in_time=2024:01:18 17:01:01.623392 -> CommitLogArchiver truncates to seconds -> updates between 01 and 01.623392 silently missing
medium.reason: Operator-visible silent data loss: 'it will silently truncate everything after the 01 seconds ... effectively to the user, it is missing updates between 01 and 01.623392'; derivable from the explicit config value given. Single-node (configure archiver, write data, restore to a sub-second timestamp).

medium.observable_symptom: Hint silently not recorded after a node goes down a second time, so 'the mutation never arrives at the node after it comes up again' (data loss / lost write)
medium.one_line_trigger: node down -> hints replayed -> node down again after > max_hint_window -> no hint file created -> mutation lost
medium.reason: Real data-delivery defect: 'the hint is not recorded, no hint file is created, and the mutation never arrives at the node after it comes up again' due to stale earliestHintByHost map not cleared. Reproduction needs a 2-node ring with repeated downtime across max_hint_window; stageable via gossip isolation / node stop-start, mechanism described but no verbatim script.

medium.observable_symptom: Node instability under load: requests pile up in an unbounded native-transport queue, ~9.5M errors and p99 ~70s latencies, and 'our results don't time out at the requested server time'
medium.one_line_trigger: mix large-read + small-read workloads on one node -> unbounded native-transport queue -> millions of errors, requests don't honor server timeout
medium.reason: Operator-visible instability with errors: 'hundreds of thousands of requests can show up in the native transport queue' and 'it can take way longer to timeout than is configured', with millions of Errors in the run table. Reproducer (easy-cass-stress commands on a single node) is in-body, but the instability/load-shedding symptom is non-deterministic -> medium.

medium.observable_symptom: Buffer overread / misaligned protocol read returning errors to the client when a warning string contains a 4-byte (supplementary-plane) UTF-8 codepoint: 'the incorrect length results in a buffer overread, which offsets the following reads, resulting in a few different possible errors'
medium.one_line_trigger: query containing U+10FFFF that triggers a warning (e.g. tombstone warning echoing the query) -> miscounted UTF-8 length -> protocol buffer overread error to client
medium.reason: Protocol-level defect returning errors to the client: encodedUTF8Length miscounts supplementary-plane chars so 'the incorrect length results in a buffer overread ... resulting in a few different possible errors'. Body gives the exact codepoint, mechanism, and a warning trigger; a runnable repro is derivable (insert high-tombstone-count data + query with U+10FFFF to fire the warning).

medium.observable_symptom: Crash / failed flush + failed restart: IllegalStateException in VectorPostings.computeRowIds aborts the SAI vector index memtable flush ('Aborting index memtable flush'), and the node 'cannot restart Cassandra'
medium.one_line_trigger: table with vector<float,1024> SAI index + map SAI index -> flush of vector index -> IllegalStateException, flush aborts, restart fails
medium.reason: Operator-visible crash: 'I cannot restart Cassandra. It'll break with the following error' and an IllegalStateException at VectorPostings.computeRowIds aborting the SAI vector-index flush. Body gives the exact CREATE TABLE + two SAI indexes (vector + map entries); inserts come via llama-index so the precise data trigger isn't pinned -> derivable, medium.

medium.observable_symptom: UnsupportedOperationException thrown from LeveledCompactionTask#partialCompactionsAcceptable when an LCS node has limited disk space and cannot drop any L0/L1 sstable
medium.one_line_trigger: LCS table + low free disk so reduceScopeForLimitedSpace removes nothing -> partialCompactionsAcceptable throws UnsupportedOperationException
medium.reason: db_behavior=true: "LeveledCompactionTask#partialCompactionsAcceptable will throw UnsupportedOperationException" when no L0/L1 sstable can be removed under limited disk; no verbatim script but schema+condition (LCS + constrained disk during compaction) is given, constructible on a single node by filling the data volume.

medium.observable_symptom: StorageService JMX MBean is not registered during bootstrap, so nodetool netstats/status/etc. on the bootstrapping node fail with InstanceNotFoundException (operator loses visibility during bootstrap)
medium.one_line_trigger: Run nodetool status/netstats on a node while it is bootstrapping -> InstanceNotFoundException org.apache.cassandra.db:type=StorageService
medium.reason: Operator-visible failure (not cosmetic output): "causes commands like 'nodetool nestats/status/etc' to not be available on the boostrapping node" with InstanceNotFoundException; reproducible by bootstrapping a new node into a ring and invoking nodetool during bootstrap. Counterweight: it is JMX MBean registration timing, which could be read as JMX internals (->false); I take true on the operator-blindness effect, hence medium.

medium.observable_symptom: privilege escalation — permissions can be granted on system keyspaces and GRANT ... ON ALL KEYSPACES wrongly includes system keyspaces (CVE-2025-23015)
medium.one_line_trigger: GRANT ... ON ALL KEYSPACES (or directly on a system keyspace) -> unauthorized access to system keyspaces
medium.reason: Authorization defect / CVE-2025-23015: must 'Restrict which permissions can be granted on system keyspaces' and 'Ensure that GRANT... ON ALL KEYSPACES excludes system keyspaces'. No verbatim script but the operations (GRANT ON ALL KEYSPACES / on system keyspaces) are derivable; single-node via cqlsh with auth enabled.

medium.observable_symptom: broken SAI query construction / wrong results for indexed reversed clustering columns of inet, bigint, varint, decimal (fixed-length byte-comparable types)
medium.one_line_trigger: SAI index on a reversed (DESC) clustering key of type inet/bigint/varint/decimal -> terms not reversed, query construction/post-filter assumptions break
medium.reason: Correctness defect: for inet/bigint/varint/decimal indexed 'as a reversed clustering key ... we don't write terms as reversed comparable bytes, and this breaks some assumptions during query construction and post-filtering'. No verbatim script but schema+operation derivable (CREATE TABLE with DESC clustering of these types + SAI index + query); single-node.

medium.observable_symptom: audit_logging_options categories loaded from cassandra.yaml on startup are not trimmed (e.g. ' ERROR', ' AUTH'), so audit-log filtering silently does not work as the operator expects.
medium.one_line_trigger: cassandra.yaml audit included_categories with spaces -> startup keeps ' ERROR'/' AUTH', filtering misbehaves
medium.reason: Body: spaces "are remaining: \" ERROR\", \" AUTH\" after parsing" so "audit logs filtering works not in a way as a user may expect and it is hard to troubleshoot"; valid config is mis-parsed at startup (operator-facing security feature), not validation hardening. Repro derivable from the shown yaml.

medium.observable_symptom: Configuring CassandraCIDRAuthorizer with parameters fails because it lacks a Map<String,String> constructor required by ParameterizedClass.newInstance, causing an instantiation/startup failure.
medium.one_line_trigger: CassandraCIDRAuthorizer with parameters -> newInstance fails (no Map constructor)
medium.reason: Body: switch to "ParameterizedClass.newInstance... requires us to have a Map<String, String>-constructor for the parameters, which CassandraCIDRAuthorizer doesn't have"; operator-visible config/startup failure, repro derivable from configuring the authorizer with params.

medium.observable_symptom: Client driver throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException (e.g. reading result kind 512) when decoding a server response because track-warnings UTF8 serialization size is off by one.
medium.one_line_trigger: response carrying track-warnings multi-byte UTF8 -> off-by-1 size -> client AIOOBE decoding result
medium.reason: Body shows driver "java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 512 out of bounds" caused by "an off-by-1 issue with track warnings serialization where the estimated size and the actual size don't match"; client-visible, but the trigger (a warning with multi-byte UTF8) must be constructed from the described mechanism.
